items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** `KeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy` is `null` after deserialization when fetching a key from Managed HSM using `Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys` 4.9.0, causing a `NullReferenceException` on `.ToString()`. This is a regression from 4.5.0.

---

<details>
<summary>🔍 Root Cause Analysis</summary>

### Most Likely Culprit: Default Service Version Bump in 4.9.0

The [4.9.0 changelog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/CHANGELOG.md) states:

> The default service version is now `2025-07-01`.

Previous GA default was `7.6` (set in 4.8.0). This version bump is the most likely source of the regression.

### How the deserialization works

In [`KeyReleasePolicy.cs`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/src/KeyReleasePolicy.cs), `EncodedPolicy` is only populated when the JSON response contains a `data` property:

```csharp
case DataPropertyName:
    byte[] data = Base64Url.Decode(prop.Value.GetString());
    EncodedPolicy = new BinaryData(data);
    break;
```

If the service response omits `data` (or the field name changed in the `2025-07-01` API), `EncodedPolicy` remains `null` after deserialization — leading directly to the reported `NullReferenceException`.

</details>

---

<details>
<summary>🐛 Reproduction Steps</summary>

```csharp
string keyName = "someKeyName";
string? version = null;

// Client targeting a Managed HSM endpoint
KeyClient client = new KeyClient(new Uri("https://(your-mhsm).managedhsm.azure.net"), new DefaultAzureCredential());

KeyVaultKey keyVaultKey = await client.GetKeyAsync(keyName, version).ConfigureAwait(false);

// ReleasePolicy is non-null (the check passes)
KeyReleasePolicy hsmKeyReleasePolicy = keyVaultKey.Properties.ReleasePolicy;
if (hsmKeyReleasePolicy == null)
    throw new InvalidOperationException($"Failed to get release policy for key: ({keyName})");

// EncodedPolicy is null — NullReferenceException thrown here
hsmKeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy.ToString();
```

</details>

---

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Debugging Strategies</summary>

1. **Confirm the service version is the trigger** — Force 4.9.0 to use the old service version:
   ```csharp
   var options = new KeyClientOptions(KeyClientOptions.ServiceVersion.V7_6);
   KeyClient client = new KeyClient(vaultUri, credential, options);
   ```
   If this eliminates the `NullReferenceException`, the `2025-07-01` API is the culprit.

2. **Inspect the raw HTTP response** — Enable logging to capture the JSON payload returned by the service and check whether the `data` field is present in the `release_policy` object.
   ```csharp
   var options = new KeyClientOptions { Diagnostics = { IsLoggingContentEnabled = true } };
   ```

3. **Compare JSON responses** — Check if `2025-07-01` changed the mHSM `release_policy` JSON field name (e.g., `data` → something else) or omits it in certain scenarios.

4. **Check for null before calling `ToString()`** — As a short-term workaround:
   ```csharp
   var policyString = hsmKeyReleasePolicy.EncodedPolicy?.ToString() ?? "(null policy data)";
   ```

</details>

---

<details>
<summary>✅ Suggested Sub-tasks</summary>

- [ ] Reproduce the issue against the `2025-07-01` service version with an mHSM key that has a release policy set.
- [ ] Compare the raw JSON response bodies for `7.6` vs `2025-07-01` for the same key.
- [ ] If the API version changed the response shape, update `KeyReleasePolicy.ReadProperties` to handle the new field name/structure.
- [ ] If `data` can legitimately be absent in the response, consider making `EncodedPolicy` null-safe or documenting this as a valid state.
- [ ] Add a unit or live test covering deserialization of a `KeyReleasePolicy` with and without the `data` field.
- [ ] Verify whether the same regression exists for non-mHSM Key Vault keys with release policies.

</details>

---

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [`KeyReleasePolicy.cs`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/src/KeyReleasePolicy.cs) — Deserialization logic
- [mHSM Release Policy Grammar]((aka.ms/redacted)
- [Azure Key Vault Release Policy Grammar]((aka.ms/redacted)
- [4.9.0 Changelog](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Keys/CHANGELOG.md)

</details>

CC: `@Azure/azure-sdk-write-keyvault`
